title: Operation
description:
An Operation is a Resource.Action pair — the atomic unit of business activity. It represents a complete statement of what can happen in the domain: Loan.Approve, Order.Ship, Employee.Terminate. The Actor that performs an Operation is expressed through Rules (access) and Tasks (assignment), not on the Operation itself.

Operation replaces the prior 'Capability' primitive. The rename avoids overlap with the Business Capability Modeling vocabulary (which uses 'capability' for coarse org functions) and aligns with Product Core's existing 'Operation' name, unifying terminology across layers.

resource.description: The Resource this operation belongs to.

action.description: The Action to be performed on the Resource.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this operation represents.

examples.name: Order.Ship
examples.description: Ships a fulfilled order to the customer.
